title: Misra-Gries: heavy hitters (> n/k) with k-1 counters
statement_informal: The Misra-Gries summary generalizes Boyer-Moore: with only k-1 counters it finds every element occurring more than n/k times in one streaming pass (k=2 is majority). On each element: increment if stored, else store if room, else decrement all counts (drop zeros). Guarantee: any m with k*cnt(m) > length keeps a positive counter.

cost_model.notes: One fold_left over the stream with a dictionary of <= k-1 (item,count) pairs. The generalized cancellation invariant: cnt x prefix <= getc x dict + D and total dict + k*D = length prefix (D = #decrement steps), so k*D <= length and a > n/k element survives.

references: J. Misra and D. Gries, 'Finding repeated elements' (Science of Computer Programming, 1982).
Generalizes work-units/boyer-moore-majority (the k=2 case); a building block of streaming heavy-hitters sketches.
